I need to understand the full setup. The user wants:
- bodyandmindon.cz = main marketing site (all important info)
- app.bodyandmindon.cz = app with only registration and profile, so /start should be the main entry (registration) and profile

This could mean:
1. **Single Next.js app** – routing by domain: if host is bodyandmindon.cz show marketing index, if app.bodyandmindon.cz redirect to /start or show app shell. That requires middleware or getServerSideProps checking req.headers.host.
2. **Or** they might deploy two separate things – one static/marketing site at bodyandmindon.cz and one app at app.bodyandmindon.cz. In that case we just need to make sure:
   - The marketing site (index) has ALL the content from bodyandmindon.cz (how it works, pricing, testimonials, CTA to app)
   - The app at app.bodyandmindon.cz has /start (registration) and /profil; maybe index of app redirects to /start or /login

I'll assume one codebase that can be deployed to both domains (or one deployment where bodyandmindon.cz and app.bodyandmindon.cz point to the same app). Then:
- When user is on bodyandmindon.cz → show full marketing landing (we need to expand index.js with all the content from the website description).
- When user is on app.bodyandmindon.cz → show app: index could redirect to /start (or /login if logged in to /profil). Pages under app: /start, /login, /profil, etc.

So the clean approach:
1. **Expand `pages/index.js`** to be the full marketing landing page with all sections from bodyandmindon.cz (hero, jak to funguje, tréninkový plán a jídelníček, motivace a pokrok, reference, ceník, FAQ, CTA). Link "Začni" should point to app: https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start (or we use /start and rely on them deploying app on subdomain so that same codebase's /start is app.bodyandmindon.cz/start).
2. **Add middleware or logic** so that when the app is accessed via app.bodyandmindon.cz, the root (/) redirects to /start (registration) or to /profil when logged in. And when accessed via bodyandmindon.cz, the root shows the marketing page.

Actually re-reading again: "tohle je hlavni stranka kde budou vsechny dulezite infoormace a na teto https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start bude jen registrace a proofil tak to upprav"

So:
- Main site (bodyandmindon.cz): all important information – so index should have the full landing content.
- app.bodyandmindon.cz/start: only registration and profile – so on the app subdomain, /start is the entry (registration), and there's profile. So we need to:
  1. Make the main landing (index) full and rich, with links to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start for "Začni".
  2. Optionally add middleware so that on app.bodyandmindon.cz, "/" redirects to "/start".

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Now I understand. The project already references:
- https://bodyandmindon.cz - main site (obchodni podminky, gdpr, footer)
- https://app.bodyandmindon.cz - app (login, profile)

The user wants:
1. **bodyandmindon.cz** = hlavní stránka se VŠEMI důležitými informacemi (obsah z popisu webu: Jak to funguje, Tréninkový plán a jídelníček, Motivace a pokrok, Reference, Ceník, FAQ, CTA). So I need to significantly expand index.js.
2. **app.bodyandmindon.cz/start** = jen registrace a profil. So on the app domain, we might want the root to redirect to /start, and the app should only expose /start (registration) and /profil (and /login). Pricing could stay on main site or be linked from main site.

Strategy:
1. **Expand pages/index.js** into a full marketing landing page with:
   - Hero (already there, refine text to match bodyandmindon.cz)
   - Jak to funguje (expand with 3 steps)
   - Tréninkový plán a jídelníček
   - Motivace a pokrok (denní výzvy, odznaky, mindset, připomínky)
   - Reference (Petra, Martin, Tereza, Jakub)
   - Ceník (START, ON Club, VIP) - can embed or link to /pricing
   - Jak funguje START program (4 kroky)
   - FAQ
   - CTA
   All "Začni" / registrace links should point to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start (use env NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL or constant).

2. **Header/Footer** on the main site (index) should link to app for Registrace, Profil, Přihlášení - so we need to use absolute URLs for app links when we're on the main site. Actually the user said "na teto https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start bude jen registrace a profil" - so the main site is bodyandmindon.cz and the app is app.bodyandmindon.cz. So:
   - On bodyandmindon.cz: Header links "Registrace" → https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start, "Profil" → https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/profil, "Přihlášení" → https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/login. "Ceník" and "Jak to funguje" can stay on main site (anchor #jak-to-funguje or /pricing on main site).
   - We might have two deployments: one for bodyandmindon.cz (marketing) and one for app.bodyandmindon.cz (app). Or one deployment with middleware. If one deployment serves both domains, we need middleware: when host === 'bodyandmindon.cz' show marketing index and maybe don't serve /start, /profil on that host (redirect to app). When host === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz' serve app (start, profil, login) and redirect / to /start.

   Simpler approach: **one codebase, one deployment**. Both domains point to same Next.js app. Then:
   - When user visits bodyandmindon.cz → they see the marketing page at /. When they click "Registrace" or "Začni", we send them to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start.
   - When user visits app.bodyandmindon.cz → we could redirect / to /start so the "app" home is the start/registration page. And /profil, /login work as now.
   - So we need middleware: if (host === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz' && pathname === '/') redirect to '/start'.
   - And in Header we need to know which domain we're on: if we're on bodyandmindon.cz, link "Registrace" to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start; if we're on app, link to /start. We can use NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL or check in getServerSideProps / client-side window.location.origin. Actually for Header we can use a constant APP_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://app.bodyandmindon.cz' and SITE_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL || 'https://bodyandmindon.cz'. Then on the main site (when we want to show marketing), links to app go to APP_URL. In a single deployment, we don't know "which site we're on" unless we check host. So: use middleware to set a header or use host in _app to pass down "isAppDomain". Or simpler: always use relative links for /start, /profil, /login. So bodyandmindon.cz/start and app.bodyandmindon.cz/start - if both domains serve the same app, then bodyandmindon.cz/start would also show the start page. The user said "na app.bodyandmindon.cz/start bude jen registrace a profil" - so they want the START page and profile ONLY on the app subdomain. So the main domain should NOT have /start and /profil - or should redirect them to app. So:
   - bodyandmindon.cz/ → marketing
   - bodyandmindon.cz/start → redirect to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start
   - bodyandmindon.cz/profil → redirect to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/profil
   - bodyandmindon.cz/login → redirect to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/login
   - bodyandmindon.cz/pricing → marketing ceník (or keep on main)
   - app.bodyandmindon.cz/ → redirect to /start
   - app.bodyandmindon.cz/start → registration
   - app.bodyandmindon.cz/profil → profile
   - app.bodyandmindon.cz/login → login

   This requires middleware that checks the host and pathname and redirects accordingly. And we need two deployments OR one deployment that handles both domains with middleware. Let me implement:
   - Middleware: 
     - If host is bodyandmindon.cz (or www.bodyandmindon.cz) and path is /start, /profil, /login → redirect to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz same path.
     - If host is app.bodyandmindon.cz and path is / → redirect to /start.
   - Index page: full marketing content (for bodyandmindon.cz or when visiting / on app we redirect so we never see index on app).
   - Header: link "Registrace" to app URL for start, "Profil" to app URL for profil, "Přihlášení" to app URL for login. We can use env NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL. When on app domain, we can still use relative /start, /profil, /login. When on main domain, we need absolute app URL. So in Header we need to know current origin. On client we have window.location.origin. So we could do: const appUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://app.bodyandmindon.cz'; const isApp = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.hostname === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz'; then Registrace href = isApp ? '/start' : appUrl + '/start'. That way one codebase works for both.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

So we have Pricing.tsx. The pricing page imports Pricing from '../components/Pricing' - so it's the .tsx. I'll build the full landing in index.js and optionally link to #cenik on the same page or keep /pricing on main site. For the main site we need to either include pricing section in index or link to /pricing. I'll add a full landing with all sections and use anchor #cenik on the same page with inline pricing (simplified cards). If they have a separate pricing page we can link "Ceník" in header to /pricing - on main domain /pricing shows pricing, on app domain we might redirect /pricing to main site or show pricing. Per user: "hlavni stranka kde budou vsechny dulezite informace" - so pricing is on main page. I'll put a Ceník section on index with the 3 plans and CTA to app.

Let me create:
1. Middleware (next.js 12+ uses middleware.js in root) - redirect bodyandmindon.cz/start, /profil, /login to app.bodyandmindon.cz; redirect app.bodyandmindon.cz/ to /start.
2. Full index.js with all sections. Use APP_URL for "Začni" buttons.
3. Header: use getAppStartUrl() etc - on client we can use window.location. So Header needs to be client-aware: if we're on main domain, link to app for Registrace/Profil/Login; if on app, use relative. I'll use a small helper: baseUrl for app = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://app.bodyandmindon.cz'. For links that go to app we need to use baseUrl when on main site. So we need to know "are we on main site?". In SSR we don't have window - we have req. So in Header we could use a prop or context. Simpler: in Header use <a href={appUrl + '/start'> for Registrace when we want to always send to app. So on main site we want Registrace → app.bodyandmindon.cz/start. On app site we want Registrace → /start (same page). So we need conditional: if (typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.hostname === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz') use relative /start; else use full app URL. But that causes hydration if server renders different. So better: always use full URL for "app" links from the header: Registrace = APP_URL/start, Profil = APP_URL/profil, Login = APP_URL/login. When you're on app, clicking Registrace going to full app URL is still the same domain so it's fine. So we can set in Header: const APP_URL = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL || 'https://app.bodyandmindon.cz'; and use <a href={`${APP_URL}/start`}> for Registrace (or Link with legacyBehavior and pass href). Actually Link with external URL we need to use <a> or Link with full href. So I'll use <a href={`${APP_URL}/start`}>Registrace</a> etc. for those three. And for "Jak to funguje" and "Ceník" - on main site they stay on main site. So link to /#jak-to-funguje and /pricing or #cenik. I'll put ceník section on index so link is /#cenik. And logo "/" - on main site goes to /, on app goes to / which we redirect to /start. Good.

4. Middleware: Next.js 13+ has middleware in root. Let me check Next version.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The website content from the user's search had different pricing: START 499 Kč/měsíc after 7 days free, ON Club 1 499 Kč/měsíc, VIP 3 999 Kč/měsíc. I'll use the structure from the search result for the landing page content (START, ON Club, VIP) and keep the existing PRICING for the /pricing page or we can show both. Actually the user said "vsechny dulezite informace" from bodyandmindon.cz - so I'll use the content from the web search for the main page (START 7 dní zdarma pak 499, ON Club 1499, VIP 3999) and link CTAs to app.bodyandmindon.cz/start.

Implementing:

1. **middleware.js** (root) - redirect by host:
   - host is bodyandmindon.cz or www.bodyandmindon.cz: if path is /start, /profil, /login → redirect to https://app.bodyandmindon.cz + path
   - host is app.bodyandmindon.cz: if path is / → redirect to /start

2. **pages/index.js** - Full landing:
   - Hero (Zapni své tělo i mysl, CTA → app/start)
   - Jak to funguje (3 kroky)
   - Tréninkový plán a jídelníček
   - Motivace a pokrok (výzvy, odznaky, mindset, připomínky)
   - Reference (4 testimonials)
   - Ceník (START, ON Club, VIP) - with links to app/start
   - Jak funguje START (4 kroky)
   - FAQ
   - Final CTA

3. **Header.js** - Logo link: on main site "/", on app we'll redirect / to /start so logo can go to / (which redirects). Registrace, Profil, Přihlášení → use APP_URL so they always go to app (when on main site they need app URL; when on app same URL is fine). "Jak to funguje" → /#jak-to-funguje (main) or we could use full bodyandmindon.cz/#jak-to-funguje when on app so they see marketing. Actually when on app, "Jak to funguje" could link to https://bodyandmindon.cz/#jak-to-funguje. "Ceník" → /#cenik on main, or bodyandmindon.cz/#cenik on app. So we need two base URLs. Let me define:
   - MAIN_SITE = https://bodyandmindon.cz (or env)
   - APP_URL = https://app.bodyandmindon.cz
   In Header: if we're on app domain, link "Jak to funguje" to MAIN_SITE + '/#jak-to-funguje', "Ceník" to MAIN_SITE + '/#cenik', logo to MAIN_SITE. If we're on main domain, logo to "/", "Jak to funguje" to "/#jak-to-funguje", "Ceník" to "/#cenik", and Registrace/Profil/Login to APP_URL/start, APP_URL/profil, APP_URL/login.
   So Header needs to know current host. That's only available on client (window.location.hostname) or in middleware. We could pass a prop from _app or use a custom hook. Easiest: use useEffect + useState to set isAppDomain and then render links. Or we use getInitialProps in _app to pass host from req. For Next 14 we could get host in getServerSideProps if we use it in a page, but Header is in layout. So client-side check in Header: const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false); useEffect(() => setMounted(true), []); const isApp = mounted && typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.hostname === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz'; Then if isApp: logo href = MAIN_SITE, Jak to funguje = MAIN_SITE + '/#jak-to-funguje', Ceník = MAIN_SITE + '/#cenik', Registrace = APP_URL + '/start' (or just /start), Profil = /profil, Login = /login. If !isApp: logo = /, Jak = /#jak-to-funguje, Ceník = /#cenik, Registrace = APP_URL + '/start', Profil = APP_URL + '/profil', Login = APP_URL + '/login'. So when on main site we need full APP_URL for app links. When on app we need full MAIN_SITE for marketing links and relative for app links. Let me implement Header with this logic.
</details></think>

| Web search results for query: Next.js 14 middleware redirect NextResponse |  | Links:<br/>1. [Redirect in Middleware in Next.js 14](https://jaketrent.com/post/redirect-middleware-next-14/)<br/>2. [Functions: NextResponse \| Next.js](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/next-response)<br/>3. [Routing: Middleware \| Next.js](https://nextjs.org/docs/14/app/building-your-application/routing/middleware)<br/>4. [Using NextResponse.rewrite in middleware of Nextjs version 14 (using app router)](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/77966483/using-nextresponse-rewrite-in-middleware-of-nextjs-version-14-using-app-router)<br/><br/># Next.js 14 Middleware Redirect with NextResponse<br/><br/>## Basic Setup<br/><br/>To redirect in Next.js 14 middleware, create a `middleware.ts` file in your project root and use `NextResponse.redirect()`:[1][3]<br/><br/>```ts<br/>import \{ NextResponse \} from 'next/server'<br/>import type \{ NextRequest \} from 'next/server'<br/><br/>export function middleware(request: NextRequest) \{<br/>  return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/home', request.url))<br/>\}<br/><br/>export const config = \{<br/>  matcher: '/about/:path*',<br/>\}<br/>```<br/><br/>## Key Points<br/><br/>**Using NextResponse.redirect():**<br/>- You must import both `NextResponse` and `NextRequest` from `'next/server'`[1][3]<br/>- The redirect must be **returned** from the middleware function to take effect[1]<br/>- If no redirect is needed, return `NextResponse.next()` to continue routing[1]<br/><br/>**URL Construction:**<br/>- Pass a `new URL()` object to `NextResponse.redirect()`, constructed from the request URL[2]<br/>- You can modify the URL before redirecting, such as adding query parameters:[2]<br/>```ts<br/>const loginUrl = new URL('/login', request.url)<br/>loginUrl.searchParams.set('from', request.nextUrl.pathname)<br/>return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl)<br/>```<br/><br/>**Path Matching:**<br/>- Use the `config.matcher` export to specify which routes trigger the middleware[1][3]<br/>- Supports multiple paths with array syntax and regex patterns[3]<br/>- Without a matcher, middleware runs on all requests[1]<br/><br/>**Note:** In Next.js 14, middleware requires `NextResponse` rather than the `redirect()` function from `'next/navigation'`, which works only in Server Components and Route Handlers.[1] |

</details>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Now update the Header so that:
- On main site (bodyandmindon.cz): Logo → /, Jak to funguje → /#jak-to-funguje, Ceník → /#cenik, Registrace/Profil/Přihlášení → APP_URL/start, APP_URL/profil, APP_URL/login
- On app site (app.bodyandmindon.cz): Logo → main site (bodyandmindon.cz), Jak to funguje → main site #jak-to-funguje, Ceník → main site #cenik, Registrace → /start, Profil → /profil, Přihlášení → /login

We need to detect domain. In Next.js we can do this client-side with useEffect + window.location.hostname. To avoid hydration mismatch we can render the same links on first paint (e.g. relative) and then update. Or we can use a env that is set per deployment: NEXT_PUBLIC_IS_APP_DOMAIN. But that would require two different builds. So client-side check is better: after mount, we know the host and can use different base URLs. For SSR we don't know - so we could default to relative links for app pages (/start, /profil, /login) and relative for marketing (/#jak-to-funguje, /#cenik). When user is on bodyandmindon.cz and clicks Registrace, they get /start which middleware then redirects to app.bodyandmindon.cz/start. So actually we don't need to change Header at all for main site - /start, /profil, /login will be redirected by middleware to app! So on main site we can keep Link href="/start" etc. and middleware does the redirect. When on app, /start is the same app so relative is correct. The only thing we might want: when on app, link "Jak to funguje" and "Ceník" and logo could go to main site so users can read marketing. So:
- Logo: on app → https://bodyandmindon.cz, on main → /
- Jak to funguje: on app → https://bodyandmindon.cz/#jak-to-funguje, on main → /#jak-to-funguje
- Ceník: on app → https://bodyandmindon.cz/#cenik, on main → /#cenik
- Registrace: on app → /start, on main → /start (middleware redirects to app)
- Profil: on app → /profil, on main → /profil (middleware redirects)
- Přihlášení: on app → /login, on main → /login (middleware redirects)

So we need MAIN_SITE = https://bodyandmindon.cz for when we're on app domain (so logo and marketing links point to main site). And when on main site we use relative / for everything. Let me add to Header the logic: use state isAppDomain (default false), useEffect set true if hostname === 'app.bodyandmindon.cz'. Then if isAppDomain: logo href = MAIN_SITE, Jak = MAIN_SITE + '/#jak-to-funguje', Ceník = MAIN_SITE + '/#cenik', Registrace = /start, Profil = /profil, Login = /login. Else: logo = /, Jak = /#jak-to-funguje, Ceník = /#cenik, Registrace = /start, Profil = /profil, Login = /login. So we only need to change logo, Jak, Ceník when on app - they should point to main site. I'll use MAIN_SITE from env: NEXT_PUBLIC_MAIN_SITE_URL || 'https://bodyandmindon.cz'.
</details></think>

Úpravy jsou hotové. Shrnutí:

---

## 1. **Hlavní stránka (bodyandmindon.cz)** – `pages/index.js`

- **Hero** – „Zapni své tělo i mysl“, úvodní text, CTA → odkaz na **app.bodyandmindon.cz/start**.
- **Jak to funguje** – 3 kroky (kvíz → AI plán → sledování pokroku).
- **Tréninkový plán a jídelníček** – jídelníček, trénink, ukázka dne.
- **Motivace a pokrok** – denní výzvy, odznaky, mindset, připomínky.
- **Reference** – 4 citace (Petra, Martin, Tereza, Jakub).
- **Ceník** – START (7 dní zdarma, pak 499 Kč), ON Club (1 499 Kč), VIP (3 999 Kč) s odkazy na **app.bodyandmindon.cz/start**.
- **Jak funguje START** – 4 kroky + CTA.
- **FAQ** – 6 otázek a odpovědí.
- **Finální CTA** – „Začni hned“ → app.

Všechny tlačítka typu „Začni“ / „Registrace“ vedou na **`https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/start`** (nebo na `NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL` z env).

---

## 2. **Aplikace (app.bodyandmindon.cz)** – middleware

- **`middleware.js`** v kořeni projektu:
  - Na **bodyandmindon.cz**: při návštěvě `/start`, `/profil` nebo `/login` → **302 přesměrování** na **app.bodyandmindon.cz/start** (resp. `/profil`, `/login`).
  - Na **app.bodyandmindon.cz**: při návštěvě **/** → přesměrování na **/start** (registrace).

Na **app** tedy uživatel vidí jen **registraci (/start)** a **profil (/profil)** (+ přihlášení /login).

---

## 3. **Header** – `components/Header.js`

- **Na hlavní doméně (bodyandmindon.cz):**  
  Logo → `/`, Jak to funguje → `/#jak-to-funguje`, Ceník → `/#cenik`, Registrace/Profil/Přihlášení → relativní `/start`, `/profil`, `/login` (middleware je na main doméně přesměruje na app).
- **Na app (app.bodyandmindon.cz):**  
  Logo → **https://bodyandmindon.cz**, Jak to funguje → **bodyandmindon.cz/#jak-to-funguje**, Ceník → **bodyandmindon.cz/#cenik**, Registrace → `/start`, Profil → `/profil`, Přihlášení → `/login`.

Použitá proměnná: `NEXT_PUBLIC_MAIN_SITE_URL` (výchozí `https://bodyandmindon.cz`).

---

## 4. **Nasazení**

- Jeden Next.js projekt může obsluhovat **obě domény** (např. na Vercelu přidat bodyandmindon.cz i app.bodyandmindon.cz na stejný projekt).
- Middleware se spouští podle hlavičky `Host`, takže na **bodyandmindon.cz** běží marketingová úvodní stránka a na **app.bodyandmindon.cz** jen **/start** (a /profil, /login) s přesměrováním `/` → `/start`.

Pokud budeš chtít upravit texty, ceník nebo přidat další sekce na hlavní stránku, napiš co přesně.

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is getting a foreign key constraint error when trying to save a workout: "insert or update on table "workouts" violates foreign key constraint "workouts_user_id_fkey"". This means the `workouts` table has a foreign key on `user_id` that references another table (typically `auth.users` in Supabase), and the value being inserted for `user_id` doesn't exist in that referenced table.

Possible causes:
1. The API is using a different user reference - e.g. Supabase Auth returns `user.id` which is from `auth.users`, but the foreign key might reference a different table (e.g. a custom `public.users` or `profiles` table) that doesn't have this user.
2. The `workouts.user_id` column references `auth.users(id)` - but maybe in their Supabase project the reference is to a different table like `public.users(id)` and the auth user isn't synced there.
3. The token/session might be returning a user that doesn't exist in the referenced table (e.g. anonymous or different auth provider).

Let me check the workouts API and the schema - what does the insert use for user_id and what does the foreign key reference?
</details></think>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The migration says `user_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id)`. So the FK points to `auth.users(id)`. The API uses `user.id` from `supabaseServer.auth.getUser(token)`, which should be the same UUID from auth.users.

So why would the FK fail? Possibilities:
1. **Different schema in production** – The user's actual Supabase table might have been created with a different FK, e.g. `REFERENCES public.users(id)` or `REFERENCES public.profiles(id)`. If they have a custom `public.users` table that's populated by a trigger from auth.users, and that trigger failed or doesn't exist, then auth.users has the user but public.users doesn't – and if the FK points to public.users, the insert would fail. But the error says "workouts_user_id_fkey" – we don't know which table it references without seeing their DB.
2. **Supabase client vs service role** – When we call `supabaseServer.from('workouts').insert()`, we're using the server client. If that client uses the anon key and RLS, the insert runs as the authenticated user. But the FK check is done by the database – it only checks that the UUID exists in the referenced table. So if user.id is from auth.getUser(token), that ID should exist in auth.users. Unless...
3. **auth.getUser(token) returns a different identity** – In some setups, getUser with a JWT might return a user that was created in a different auth schema or the ID might be wrong. Unlikely.
4. **Their table references public.profiles or public.users** – Many Supabase projects have a `public.profiles` (or `public.users`) table with id REFERENCES auth.users(id), and other tables reference public.profiles(id). If workouts was created with REFERENCES public.profiles(id) but the user never got a row in public.profiles (e.g. no trigger to create profile on signup), then the insert would fail with this exact error.

The most common cause: **workouts.user_id references public.users or public.profiles**, and that table doesn't have a row for this user. So we need to either:
- Change the FK in the DB to reference auth.users(id) (migration), or
- Ensure the user exists in public.profiles / public.users (trigger or sync).

I'll add a migration that drops the existing FK (if it references the wrong table) and re-adds it to auth.users. But we can't easily "change" FK without knowing the current reference. So I'll provide:
1. A migration that ensures the FK is to auth.users(id) – we can do ALTER TABLE workouts DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS workouts_user_id_fkey; ALTER TABLE workouts ADD CONSTRAINT workouts_user_id_fkey FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
2. Documentation explaining the cause and the fix.

If their table already references auth.users and the error still happens, then the only remaining possibility is that the user.id we're sending is somehow not in auth.users (e.g. stale token, or wrong client). Let me also add a small check in the API to log the user.id so they can verify, and suggest they check in Supabase Auth that the user exists.

Actually re-reading the error again: "insert or update on table "workouts" violates foreign key constraint "workouts_user_id_fkey"" - the constraint name is workouts_user_id_fkey. So the FK is on workouts(user_id). The referenced table could be auth.users or something else. The fix that works in both cases is to make the FK point to auth.users(id), because that's where Supabase Auth stores users. So the migration I'll write:
1. Drop constraint workouts_user_id_fkey
2. Add new constraint referencing auth.users(id)

This way if they had a wrong reference (e.g. to public.users), it will be fixed. If they already had auth.users and it still fails, then the issue might be that the user doesn't exist in auth.users (e.g. custom auth, or user deleted). Let me write the migration and a short doc.
</details></think>

</details>
</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The server uses the service role key, so it can insert into workouts. The user_id comes from supabaseServer.auth.getUser(token) - that returns the user from the JWT. So user.id should be the auth.users id.

The FK violation can happen if:
1. In their actual Supabase project, the table `workouts` was created with a FK to a different table (e.g. `public.profiles(id)`) instead of `auth.users(id)`. So when we insert user.id (from auth), that UUID doesn't exist in public.profiles.
2. The JWT might be from a user that was deleted from auth.users.
3. Some Supabase versions or custom setups use a different auth schema.

The safest fix is a migration that:
- Drops the existing FK constraint on workouts(user_id)
- Adds a new FK to auth.users(id)

So the migration will be:
```sql
-- Fix: workouts.user_id must reference auth.users(id), not e.g. public.profiles
ALTER TABLE workouts DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS workouts_user_id_fkey;
ALTER TABLE workouts ADD CONSTRAINT workouts_user_id_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
```

This way, if their table was pointing to public.profiles or public.users, we fix it to point to auth.users. If the user still gets the error after this, then the problem is that the user doesn't exist in auth.users (e.g. they need to sign up again or the token is for a deleted user).

I'll also add a note in the docs about ensuring the user is actually in Auth (Supabase Dashboard → Authentication → Users).
</details></think>

---

Udělaly se dvě věci:

---

## 1. Proč to padá

Chyba **`workouts_user_id_fkey`** znamená, že sloupec **`workouts.user_id`** má cizí klíč na nějakou tabulku a **vkládané UUID tam neexistuje**.

Často je to tak, že:

- **API** bere `user.id` z **Supabase Auth** (`auth.getUser(token)`), tedy z tabulky **`auth.users`**.
- Tabulka **`workouts`** ale má cizí klíč nastavený na **jinou tabulku** (např. `public.profiles` nebo `public.users`).
- V té tabulce pro tvého přihlášeného uživatele **není záznam** → insert do `workouts` selže.

---

## 2. Migrace – na co ji spustit

Přidal jsem soubor **`supabase/migrations/20250219_fix_workouts_user_id_fkey.sql`**. Ten:

1. **Odstraní** stávající cizí klíč `workouts_user_id_fkey`.
2. **Přidá** nový cizí klíč: `workouts.user_id` → **`auth.users(id)`** (s `ON DELETE CASCADE`).

Tím bude `workouts` odkazovat přímo na uživatele z Auth, takže `user_id` z API bude v pořádku.

**Spuštění:**  
V Supabase Dashboard → **SQL Editor** vlož a spusť obsah souboru:

`supabase/migrations/20250219_fix_workouts_user_id_fkey.sql`

---

## 3. Kdyby to po migraci stále padalo

- V **Supabase → Authentication → Users** zkontroluj, že uživatel s daným e-mailem existuje (ne že byl smazaný).
- Zkus se znovu **odhlásit a přihlásit**, aby frontend poslal nový token.

V **docs/WORKOUTS_SCHEMA_ROZBOR.md** a v **ANALYZA_PROFILU.md** je k téhle chybě a migraci doplněný krátký popis pro další použití.

Spusť v Supabase tuto opravu. Udělej to takto:

---

## Krok 1: Spusť SQL v Supabase

1. Otevři **Supabase Dashboard** → tvůj projekt  
2. V levém menu zvol **SQL Editor**  
3. Vlož tento kód a klikni na **Run**:

```sql
-- Oprava FK: workouts.user_id → auth.users(id)
ALTER TABLE workouts DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS workouts_user_id_fkey;

ALTER TABLE workouts ADD CONSTRAINT workouts_user_id_fkey
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE;
```

4. Mělo by se zobrazit **Success** (bez chyby).

---

## Krok 2: Zkus znovu uložit trénink

Po úspěšném spuštění SQL zkus znovu v aplikaci **„Uložit trénink“**. Chyba by měla zmizet.

---

## Kdyby to pořád padalo

- V Supabase jdi na **Authentication → Users** a ověř, že tam máš uživatele s e-mailem, kterým se přihlašuješ.  
- Zkus se v aplikaci **odhlásit a znovu přihlásit** a pak znovu uložit trénink.

Pokud po těchto krocích chyba stále uvidíš, napiš prosím, jestli při spuštění SQL v Editoru nevyšla nějaká chybová hláška (zkopíruj ji sem).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The Vercel build is failing because when Next.js collects page data, it loads the admin page which imports something that uses supabaseServer - and supabaseServer.js throws at import time if SUPABASE_URL is missing. So even though the env vars might be set in Vercel for runtime, the build runs in a context where they're not available (or the check runs at module load time).

The fix is to make the supabaseServer module not throw during build when env vars are missing - we can lazy-initialize or only throw when the client is actually used (e.g. when an API route runs), not at import time. That way "Collecting page data" won't trigger the throw.

Let me look at supabaseServer.js again and the admin page to see the dependency chain.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: c:\Users\prikopa\Documents\GitHub\body-mind-on\pages\admin.js</summary>

</details>
</tool-use>

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The issue is that when Next.js runs "Collecting page data", it evaluates the admin page module, which imports supabaseServer. The supabaseServer module runs at import time and throws if env vars are missing. On Vercel build, the env vars might not be available during the build phase (they're often only injected at runtime), or they might not be set in the build environment.

Solution: Don't throw at import time. Create the client lazily - only when it's first used - and throw at that point if env is missing. That way:
1. During build, the module loads without throwing (we don't call createClient until someone actually uses supabaseServer).
2. When an API route or getServerSideProps runs at runtime, it will use supabaseServer and then we need the env - we can create the client on first access and throw if env is missing then.

So I'll change supabaseServer.js to export a getter that creates the client on first use, or create the client only when env is available and otherwise export a proxy that throws on use. The simplest is:

- If SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY are set, create the client as now.
- If not, export a proxy object that throws when any property is accessed (so we don't throw at import time, but we throw when actually used in runtime).

Actually the cleaner approach: create the client lazily inside a function getSupabaseServer(), and the exported supabaseServer could be a Proxy that on first property access calls getSupabaseServer() and then delegates. That way build never calls getSupabaseServer(). Or we could just not throw at import time - use empty string and let createClient be called with invalid values. Then the first actual request that uses it will fail with a more cryptic error. Better: lazy init with throw on first use.

Implementation:
let _client = null;
function getClient() {
  if (_client) return _client;
  const url = process.env.SUPABASE_URL || process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
  const key = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;
  if (!url || !key) throw new Error('...');
  _client = createClient(url, key, { auth: { persistSession: false } });
  return _client;
}
export const supabaseServer = new Proxy({}, {
  get(_, prop) {
    return getClient()[prop];
  }
});

That way when Next.js imports the module during build, no throw. When at runtime someone does supabaseServer.from('x'), getClient() is called and then we throw if env is missing. But wait - during "Collecting page data" Next.js might actually run getServerSideProps for admin in some environments? Let me check - the error says "Collecting page data" and the stack shows admin.js. So it's loading the admin page and running getServerSideProps. So at that point we're not just importing - we're actually executing getServerSideProps. So the env vars might be available during build on Vercel if they're set in Project Settings - but the user said the error is "Chybí SUPABASE_URL". So either the env vars are not set in Vercel, or they're not available during build (Vercel sometimes only injects env at runtime for serverless functions).

So we have two fixes:
1. Lazy init so that we don't throw at import time - then build will pass. When someone visits /admin at runtime, getServerSideProps will run and then we'll throw if env is missing (or we could return notFound so the page still builds).
2. Tell the user to set the env vars in Vercel - but they might be set and still not available at build time.

The safest fix is lazy init: don't throw when the module is loaded, only when the client is actually used. Then the build succeeds. If env is missing at runtime when someone accesses admin or an API that uses supabaseServer, they'll get the error then. I'll implement the Proxy approach.
</details></think>

Actually wait - during "Collecting page data" Next.js might run getServerSideProps for static generation or for the build. So for admin page, getServerSideProps is run at build time? In Next.js, getServerSideProps runs at request time, not at build time - so the page is not statically generated. So "Collecting page data" might just be loading the module to analyze it. So with lazy init, the module loads without throwing, and we're good. Let me double-check - the error stack shows the throw coming from 8026 (minified) in admin.js - so it's when admin.js is being loaded/executed, and admin.js imports supabaseServer, which runs the top-level throw. Úprava je hotová.

**Co se změnilo**

- **Předtím:** `lib/supabaseServer.js` házel chybu hned při **načtení modulu** (když chyběly `SUPABASE_URL` / `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`). Při Vercel buildu se načte i stránka `/admin`, takže build padl.
- **Teď:** Klient se vytvoří až při **prvním použití** (lazy init přes Proxy). Při samotném importu se tedy žádná chyba nevyhazuje a build proběhne.

**Co udělat**

1. Znovu nasadit na Vercel (push, nebo Redeploy).
2. V projektu na Vercelu mít nastavené env:
   - `SUPABASE_URL` nebo `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`
   - `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`  
   (Project Settings → Environment Variables; pro build stačí, když jsou dostupné aspoň při běhu.)

Build by měl projít. Když pak někdo otevře `/admin` nebo API, které používají Supabase, a env v tom prostředí chybí, chyba se objeví až v tom okamžiku.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I see the issues now:

1. **Stats calculation**: The API already computes `workouts_this_week` and `total_workouts` from the fetched workouts. The week start is computed with `weekStart.setDate(now.getDate() - now.getDay() + (now.getDay() === 0 ? -6 : 1))` - that's ISO week (Monday start). And we filter `workouts.filter(w => (w.workout_date || '') >= weekStartStr)`. The issue could be that `workout_date` from the database might be in a different format (e.g. with timezone or full timestamp) so the string comparison might fail. Let me check - the API returns workouts from Supabase; workout_date is likely a date column so it might come as "2026-02-19". And weekStartStr is toISOString().split('T')[0] so "2026-02-17" for Monday. So "2026-02-19" >= "2026-02-17" should be true. Unless the profile data is cached or the frontend is not using the latest profile after adding workouts? When the user adds a workout in the modal, we do setProfile((p) => ({ ...p, workouts: [json.workout, ...(p.workouts || [])] })) - we add the new workout to state but we don't recalculate stats! So the stats (workouts_this_week, total_workouts) come from the initial profile fetch. When we add a workout client-side we don't update profile.stats. So we need to either:
   A) Recalculate stats on the frontend when we have workouts (so when we add a workout, recalc from workouts array), or
   B) Refetch profile after adding a workout.

   Option A is simpler and ensures the UI always reflects the current workouts list. So in profil.js we should compute workouts_this_week and total_workouts from the `workouts` array (which is from profile and gets updated when we add/delete). So we need to derive stats from workouts on the client instead of using profile.stats for workouts. Let me do that.

2. **Body figure**: The user wants it to look "far better". Currently:
   - When hasBeforeAfter is true but firstMetric and latestMetric have the same weight (99 kg) and same date, both figures look identical. The condition hasBeforeAfter is (firstMetric.id !== latestMetric.id || firstMetric.weight_kg !== latestMetric.weight_kg) - so if they have the same weight but different id (two different metric entries), we still show before/after and they look the same.
   - The BodyFigure component uses bmiToBodyType(weight, height) to get a "type" that changes torso width and leg width - so same weight/height = same figure. To make "before" vs "now" look better:
     - We could make the "Předtím" figure slightly different style (e.g. grayscale, or smaller, or with a subtle "ghost" effect) so even with same numbers there's a visual distinction.
     - We could improve the SVG design: better proportions, nicer gradients, maybe a silhouette that looks more like a body.
     - We could add a visible "progress" indicator when weight changed (e.g. green border on "Teď", or a badge "-2 kg").
   - I'll improve the BodyFigure component: better shape, clearer difference between before/after (e.g. "Předtím" with muted/gray tone, "Teď" with vibrant gradient; or "Předtím" slightly smaller/opacity). And in the profil, when we have before/after with the same weight, we could still show them but with a message like "Přidej další měření, aby se zobrazil rozdíl" or we make the visual distinction by style (before = grayed, now = colored).

Let me implement:
1. In profil.js: compute workoutsThisWeek and totalWorkouts from the `workouts` array (with same week logic as API) so when user adds a workout the numbers update immediately. Use these computed values instead of stats.workouts_this_week and stats.total_workouts.
2. BodyFigure: improve the visual - more polished silhouette, and add a variant prop like variant="before" | "now" so we can style "before" as muted and "now" as vibrant. In profil pass variant to BodyFigure.
3. Optional: when firstMetric and latestMetric have same weight/height, show a hint "Zatím stejná váha – přidej měření pro zobrazení pokroku" and still style before/now differently so the figures look better (before muted, now highlighted).
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

Get the week start for "this week" (Monday). In Czech locale Monday is first day of week.
weekStartStr = Monday 00:00 of current week in YYYY-MM-DD.
const now = new Date();
const day = now.getDay(); // 0 = Sun, 1 = Mon, ...
// Monday = 1, so days since Monday = (day + 6) % 7, or if day === 0 then 6 days ago
const daysToMonday = day === 0 ? 6 : day - 1;
const weekStart = new Date(now);
weekStart.setDate(now.getDate() - daysToMonday);
const weekStartStr = weekStart.toISOString().split('T')[0];

Then workoutsThisWeek = workouts.filter(w => (w.workout_date || '').slice(0, 10) >= weekStartStr).length;
And totalWorkouts = workouts.length;

We need to normalize workout_date - it might come as full ISO string from DB, so .slice(0, 10) to get YYYY-MM-DD.
</details></think>

Shrnutí změn:

---

## 1. **Automatický výpočet statistik**

- **Tréninků tento týden** a **Celkem tréninků** se teď počítají v prohlížeči z aktuálního seznamu `workouts`.
- Po přidání nebo smazání tréninku se čísla hned přepočítají, bez znovunačítání stránky nebo profilu.
- Týden je od pondělí (ISO týden), takže „tento týden“ odpovídá běžnému zobrazení.

---

## 2. **Vylepšená postava (Předtím vs. Teď)**

- **Předtím:** Šedá silueta (tlumené barvy), mírně snížená výraznost, aby působila jako „starý stav“.
- **Teď:** Fialový gradient, lehký glow a zvýrazněná karta (fialový okraj + stín), aby byl aktuální stav jasně zdůrazněný.
- **Tvar postavy:** Jednodušší, zaoblená silueta (hlava, krk, trup, paže, nohy) s plynulejším přechodem mezi částmi těla.
- Karta „Teď“ má fialový rámeček a jemný stín, karta „Předtím“ zůstává neutrální.

Po uložení tréninku by se měla hned zvýšit hodnota „Tréninků tento týden“ a „Celkem tréninků“, a postavy „Předtím“ a „Teď“ by měly být vizuálně lépe odlišené.

The user has three concerns:

1. **Why doesn't the weight change?** - The display shows 99 kg for both "Předtím" and "Teď" with the same date. So either they only have one body_metrics record, or the logic for "first" vs "latest" is showing the same data. The weight changes when the user adds NEW body metrics (via "Aktualizovat metriky" / the start form). So I need to: (a) make it clear in the UI that weight comes from "měření" (body_metrics) and they need to add new measurements over time for the weight to change; (b) maybe add a "quick add weight" so they can just log weight+date without full form; (c) check the logic - firstMetric vs latestMetric: firstMetric = metrics[metrics.length - 1] (oldest), latestMetric = metrics[0] (newest). If they have only ONE metric entry, then firstMetric and latestMetric are the SAME object, so hasBeforeAfter would be firstMetric.id !== latestMetric.id || firstMetric.weight_kg !== latestMetric.weight_kg - for one record, id is same and weight is same, so hasBeforeAfter would be FALSE. So they'd see the "single" view (Tvůj profil). But the image says they see "Předtím" and "Teď" both with 99 kg and same date - so they must have two body_metrics entries with the same weight and possibly same date. So the weight doesn't "change" because they haven't entered a different weight in any measurement - they have two entries with 99 kg. So the fix for "why doesn't weight change" is: (1) Explain in UI that they need to add new measurements with different dates/weights to see progress; (2) Add a quick way to add just weight (and date) so they can log weight easily over time.

2. **The body figure is still not what they imagined** - They want a more interesting, appealing body visualization. So I need to redesign BodyFigure to be more engaging: perhaps a more realistic silhouette, or a more attractive/stylized one that people find interesting. Could use: better proportions, a more "fitness" style figure, or something more illustrative.

3. **Make it interesting for people** - So the whole section should be more engaging: clearer explanation of how to see progress, maybe a motivational line, and a body figure that looks more appealing/professional.

Let me:
1. Check how body_metrics / weight_history work and where weight is displayed - and add a short hint that "Váha se mění podle záznamů měření. Přidej nové měření s jinou váhou, aby se zobrazil pokrok."
2. Add a "Rychlé přidání váhy" (quick add weight) - a small form or link that lets them add just date + weight (could be a modal or inline) - so they don't have to go through full start form. I need to check if there's an API for that - body-metrics might expect full form. Let me check.
3. Redesign BodyFigure to be more appealing - maybe a cleaner, more modern silhouette, or a style that looks more "fitness app". I could try a more proportional human shape, or use CSS to make it look more polished.

Let me check the body-metrics API and start form to see what's needed for adding weight.
</details></think>

From the payload in body-metrics.js: email, name, gender, age, height_cm, weight_kg, activity, stress_level, occupation, goal, freq_choice, notes, program, created_at, user_id. So the table might have many columns. For a "quick add weight" we'd need to either have a separate simple table (weight_log) or insert into body_metrics with required fields. If body_metrics has NOT NULL constraints on many columns, we need to copy from the last metric. Let me check if there's a quick weight API or we need to create one.

1. Why doesn't the weight change? → Because they need to ADD NEW measurements. The app shows "Předtím" = first measurement, "Teď" = latest. If both are 99 kg it's because either they have one measurement, or two measurements both with 99 kg. So the solution is: (a) make it clear in the UI that to see weight change they need to add new measurements with a new weight; (b) add a quick way to add just weight (and date) so it's easy to log regularly.

Add an API route POST /api/body-metrics/quick (or add to profile API) that for authenticated user accepts { weight_kg, date? } and inserts into body_metrics using user_id from token and copying height_cm (and other required fields) from the user's latest body_metrics row. I need to check what columns body_metrics has - the insert in body-metrics.js sends payload with many fields. So the table likely has: user_id, email, name, gender, age, height_cm, weight_kg, activity, stress_level, occupation, goal, freq_choice, weekly_sessions_user, notes, program, created_at. Some might be nullable. For quick add we'd do: get last body_metrics for user, then insert new row with same everything except weight_kg (and maybe created_at for the date they chose). So we need an endpoint that: gets user from token, gets latest body_metrics for user, inserts new row with weight_kg from request and rest copied from latest (or minimal required set).

So: start at top of head, draw head (ellipse), then go to left shoulder, down left side, left leg, between legs, right leg, right side up to right shoulder, close. Let me simplify - use a single path that describes the outline. The path I wrote has "M cx headR a headR headR*1.1" which is an arc for the head - but that's not a full circle, and then M starts a new path. In SVG, when you have multiple subpaths in one path element, they all get filled together (fill-rule). So the body might have a hole in the head area. Let me draw it differently: one continuous outline. Start at top of head (cx, 0), go around head, down neck to shoulder, etc. I'll use a simpler approach: keep the path as one closed shape - head as circle, then from bottom of circle go to left shoulder, down left side, left leg, between legs, right leg, right side, back to right shoulder and to head. So the path order should be: head circle (or arc), then body outline. Actually the simplest is: path d = head ellipse + body path. But in SVG one path with two disconnected closed shapes still fills both. So we get head filled and body filled. The issue is the "M" moves to start a new subpath - so we have head (circle) and body (the rest). Both get filled. Good. But the body part I wrote - "M cx - shoulderW shoulderY Q ... Z" - the Z closes back to cx-shoulderW, shoulderY. So we have a closed body. But the body doesn't include the neck - so there might be a gap between head and body. Let me redraw so the figure looks like one connected silhouette: head (circle), then a trapezoid/body from neck to hips, then two legs. I'll simplify the path so it's one connected outline.

Actually looking at my path again - the head is "M cx headR a headR headR*1.1 0 1 1 0.01 0" - that's an arc that draws a full circle (or ellipse) centered at cx, headR with radius headR. Wait, "a" is arc: rx ry x-axis-rotation large-arc sweep x y. So the arc goes from current point to (cx+0.01, headR). The current point after M cx headR is (cx, headR). So we're drawing an arc from (cx, headR) to (cx+0.01, headR). That's almost a full circle - good. Then "M cx - shoulderW shoulderY" starts the body. So we have two closed shapes - head and body. The body goes from left shoulder down left side, left leg, between legs, right leg, right side, back to right shoulder, Z. So the body is closed. But the head and body are separate - there's a gap (neck). So the silhouette might look like a head and a body with a gap. Let me connect them: from bottom of head circle go to left shoulder, then body, then right shoulder, then back to bottom of head. So one path: start at top of head, draw head (circle), at bottom of head we're at (cx, headR*2). Then line to (cx-shoulderW, shoulderY), then body outline down and around, then (cx+shoulderW, shoulderY), then line back to (cx, headR*2) and close. That way head and body are one connected shape. Let me rewrite the path properly.

Actually for a quick fix, let me just make the path one continuous outline. Start at top of head (cx, 0). <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

</details></think>

---


Shrnutí změn:

---

## 1. **Proč se váha neměnila a jak to funguje teď**

- **Váha** jde ze **záznamů měření** (body_metrics). „Předtím“ = první měření, „Teď“ = poslední. Pokud máš jen jeden záznam nebo dva se stejnou váhou (třeba 99 kg), čísla i postava zůstanou stejné.
- **Přidáno:**
  - Krátké **vysvětlení** v sekci „Tvůj postup“: že váha a postava vycházejí z měření a že nové měření s jinou váhou přepočítá pokrok.
  - **Rychlé přidání váhy:** tlačítko **„Přidat váhu“** v Rychlých akcích a **modal** (datum + váha v kg). Po uložení se profil znovu načte a přepočítá se postava, graf i „Změní od začátku“.

---

## 2. **API pro rychlé přidání váhy**

- **POST `/api/quick-weight`** – pro přihlášeného uživatele ukládá nový záznam do `body_metrics` (váha + volitelně datum). Ostatní pole (výška, e-mail, …) se zkopírují z posledního měření, takže stačí jen váha.

---

## 3. **Postava – atraktivnější a srozumitelnější**

- **Nový tvar:** Jedna plynulá silueta (kruh hlavy + trup a nohy v jedné cestě). Šířka trupu a nohou závisí na BMI (nižší BMI = štíhlejší, vyšší = mohutnější), takže změna váhy je na postavě lépe vidět.
- **Předtím vs. Teď:** „Předtím“ je v tlumené šedé, „Teď“ v živější fialové s jemným glow.
- **Rozdíl váhy u „Teď“:** Pokud se váha oproti „Předtím“ změnila, u postavy „Teď“ se zobrazí badge **„-X kg“** (zeleně při úbytku) nebo **„+X kg“** (oranžově při nárůstu).

---

## 4. **Co má uživatel dělat**

1. V **Rychlých akcích** kliknout na **„Přidat váhu“**.
2. Zadat **datum** a **váhu** (např. po týdnu 97 kg).
3. **Uložit** – profil se znovu načte, postava „Teď“ se přepočítá, „Změní od začátku“ a graf váhy se aktualizují.

Pravidelným přidáváním váhy (třeba 1× týdně) bude průběh jasně vidět a sekce bude působit živěji a srozumitelněji.

---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is saying that when they are logged in, there should only be "odhlášení" (logout) - not "Přihlášení" (Login) in the header. So when the user is authenticated, the header should show "Odhlásit se" (or similar) instead of "Registrace", "Profil", "Přihlášení". So we need to make the Header component aware of the auth state and conditionally show:
- When logged out: Jak to funguje, Ceník, Registrace, Profil?, Přihlášení
- When logged in: Jak to funguje, Ceník, Registrace (or hide?), Profil, Odhlásit se (instead of Přihlášení)

The Header is used on both the main site and the app. So we need to pass session/auth state to the Header, or the Header needs to get the session itself (e.g. from a hook or context). Let me check the Header component - it doesn't receive any props about auth. So we have two options:
1. Pass a prop like `isLoggedIn` or `session` from each page to Header. But Header is used in many pages (index, profil, start, login, etc.) and we'd need to pass session from each. That's messy.
2. Header fetches session itself (e.g. useEffect + supabase.auth.getSession()) and then shows different nav items. This way any page that uses Header will get the correct display. Let me do that.

So in Header.js we'll:
- Add state for user/session (e.g. user from getSession)
- useEffect to get session on mount
- If user is logged in: show "Profil" and "Odhlásit se" (link to logout or button that calls logout - but logout needs to redirect, so we need a way to do it. We could use a link to /api/logout or we could have a client-side logout. Actually the simplest is: when logged in, show "Odhlásit se" as a link that goes to a logout page that signs out and redirects to home, or we use onClick to sign out and redirect. So we need the Header to call supabase.auth.signOut() and then router.push('/') or window.location.href = '/'. So Header needs to use useRouter and supabase client. Let me check - Header is used in profil.js which has its own logout. So when on profil, the user sees Header with "Přihlášení" - and they want to see "Odhlásit se" instead. So the Header should show "Odhlásit se" when logged in. When clicked, we need to logout. So we need supabase in Header. And we need to know if we're on app or main domain - on app, logout might redirect to /login or /start. Let me keep it simple: when logged in, show "Odhlásit se" that calls signOut and redirects to "/" (or /login on app). We can use router.push after signOut. So Header will:
- useSession or getSession from supabase
- if session: show Odhlásit se (button or link that triggers signOut + redirect)
- if !session: show Registrace, Profil, Přihlášení

I'll also hide "Registrace" when logged in? The user said "jen odhlášení" - so they want only logout when logged in. So when logged in we show: Jak to funguje, Ceník, Profil, Odhlásit se. We hide Registrace and Přihlášení when logged in. Let me implement.
</details></think>

- **Když jsi přihlášen:** V menu se zobrazí jen **Profil** a **Odhlásit se**. Položky „Registrace“ a „Přihlášení“ se skryjí.
- **Když nejsi přihlášen:** Zůstanou **Registrace**, **Profil** a **Přihlášení**.
- **Odhlásit se:** Tlačítko v hlavičce odhlásí uživatele (Supabase `signOut`) a přesměruje na úvodní stránku nebo na `/login` na app doméně.

Header teď načítá session z Supabase a reaguje na změnu přihlášení (`onAuthStateChange`), takže po přihlášení nebo odhlášení se položky menu hned přepnou.

Úpravy jsou hotové. Tvar postavy teď vychází z toho, „jaký člověk je“ – z údajů v měření.

---

## Co se změnilo

### 1. **Pohlaví (gender)**  
Z měření se bere pohlaví (Muž / Žena). Podle toho se mění proporce siluety:
- **Muž:** širší ramena, užší boky, výraznější „V“ tvar.
- **Žena:** užší ramena, širší boky, mírně zúžený pas.

### 2. **Cíl (goal)**  
Podle cíle z dotazníku se jemně upraví tvar:
- **Nárůst svalů:** ramena trochu širší, atletičtější postava.
- **Redukce:** lehce štíhlejší proporce.

### 3. **Váha a výška (BMI)**  
Nadále platí: vyšší BMI → mohutnější silueta, nižší BMI → štíhlejší.

---

## Předávání údajů

Do komponenty postavy se z každého měření předává **gender** a **goal** (u „Předtím“ z prvního měření, u „Teď“ z posledního). Pokud uživatel v dotazníku vyplnil pohlaví a cíl, postava se podle toho zobrazí.

Text v sekci „Tvůj postup“ je upravený na:  
*„Tvar a postava se odvíjejí od tvého profilu (pohlaví, cíl) a váhy z měření…“*

Pokud v měření pohlaví nebo cíl chybí (starší záznamy), postava zůstane neutrální (stejné proporce jako dřív). Po vyplnění dotazníku nebo úpravě měření se tvar přizpůsobí.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I see the issue. 

**weight_history** is built in the API by grouping body_metrics by **date** (created_at split by 'T')[0]). So for each date we keep only ONE weight (the first one that was seen when iterating). So if the user has two measurements on the same day (19.2.2026) - one 98.6 kg and one 99 kg - weight_history will have only one entry for that date (whichever comes first in the forEach). So weightHistory might be like [{ date: '2026-02-19', weight: 98.6 }] or [{ date: '2026-02-19', weight: 99 }] - only one! So firstWeight and lastWeight would be the same → weightDiff = 0.

**firstMetric and latestMetric** are from body_metrics ordered by created_at descending: so latestMetric = metrics[0] (newest), firstMetric = metrics[metrics.length - 1] (oldest). So we have two different measurements (98.6 and 99) and they show correctly in the body figures. But weightDiff is computed from weight_history which collapses by date, so we get 0.

**Fix:** Compute weightDiff from the same source as the body figures: firstMetric.weight_kg and latestMetric.weight_kg. So:
- firstWeight = firstMetric?.weight_kg (oldest measurement)
- lastWeight = latestMetric?.weight_kg (newest measurement)
- weightDiff = lastWeight - firstWeight when both exist

And for "Aktuální váha" we already use lastWeight - we should use latestMetric.weight_kg for consistency (we already have lastWeight from weightHistory - but lastWeight could be wrong when multiple measurements per day). So let's use latestMetric.weight_kg for current weight and for weightDiff use (latestMetric.weight_kg - firstMetric.weight_kg). That way everything is tied to the same source: body_metrics (first vs latest record).

I'll also add a note that "tréninky" are connected - the counts already update when they add workouts (we did that earlier). So the only fix needed is weightDiff and ensuring "aktuální váha" and "změna od začátku" use the same logic as the body section.

Let me also consider: should the graf váhy (weight chart) use weight_history (by date) or all metrics? The chart shows weight over time - so it makes sense to show one point per date or one per measurement. Currently weight_history is one per date. So the chart can stay as is. But the KPI "Změní od začátku" and "Aktuální váha" should be from first/latest metric so they match the body figures.

Changes:
1. In profil.js: Compute weightDiff from firstMetric and latestMetric: weightDiff = (latestMetric.weight_kg != null && firstMetric.weight_kg != null) ? (latestMetric.weight_kg - firstMetric.weight_kg).toFixed(1) : null. And lastWeight for display in KPI should be latestMetric.weight_kg when available (so it matches "Teď").
2. Optionally show in the overview that tréninky a váha jsou propojené - e.g. a short line "Tréninky a váha vycházejí z tvých záznamů." or we could add a small visual link - like in the KPI card "Tréninků tento týden" show a tiny icon that suggests it's from the list below. Actually the user said "všechno propojene a graficky aby tomu taky odpovidalo" - so they want the graphics to reflect the connections. So maybe: ensure the numbers are consistent, and add a subtle visual that the overview cards relate to the sections below (e.g. same styling, or a short sentence that ties it together).

Let me implement the fix for weightDiff and lastWeight to use metrics consistently. Then I'll add a small "propojení" - e.g. in the Přehled pokroku section add one line: "Údaje vycházejí z tvých měření a záznamů tréninků." so it's clear everything is connected.
</details></think>

---


Úpravy jsou hotové. Vše je propojené a grafika s čísly sedí.

---

## 1. **Proč „Změní od začátku“ ukazovalo 0,0 kg**

- **Aktuální váha** a **Změní od začátku** se dřív braly z `weight_history`, kde je **jen jedna váha na den** (první záznam v daný den).
- Když máš v jeden den dvě měření (98,6 kg a 99 kg), v přehledu zůstala jen jedna hodnota, takže rozdíl vycházel 0.
- **Postavy** vždy používaly první a poslední **záznam měření** (ne agregaci po dnech), takže „Předtím“ a „Teď“ byly správně 98,6 vs 99.

**Úprava:**  
Aktuální váha i „Změní od začátku“ se teď počítají ze **stejného zdroje jako postavy** – z prvního a posledního záznamu v měření.  
→ „Aktuální váha“ = poslední měření (99 kg).  
→ „Změní od začátku“ = rozdíl poslední − první měření (např. +0,4 kg).  
→ Čísla v přehledu odpovídají „Předtím“ a „Teď“.

---

## 2. **Graf váhy**

- Graf teď kreslí **všechna měření** v čase (ne jednu váhu na den).
- Data pro graf jsou stejná jako pro postavy a KPI (vše z `body_metrics`).
- Pod grafem je text: *„Podle tvých záznamů měření (stejná data jako ‚Předtím‘ / ‚Teď‘).“*

---

## 3. **Tréninky a propojení**

- **Tréninků tento týden** a **Celkem tréninků** se už přepočítávají z **aktuálního seznamu tréninků** – po přidání tréninku se čísla hned změní (to už bylo).
- Pod sekcí **Přehled pokroku** je doplněný text:  
  *„Všechny údaje vycházejí z tvých záznamů: tréninky z Historie, váha a změna z měření (stejný zdroj jako postava ‚Předtím‘ / ‚Teď‘).“*

---

## Shrnutí

| Co | Zdroj |
|----|--------|
| Postava „Předtím“ / „Teď“ | první a poslední měření |
| Aktuální váha | poslední měření |
| Změní od začátku | poslední − první měření |
| Graf váhy | všechna měření v čase |
| Tréninků tento týden / Celkem | seznam tréninků (po přidání se hned přepočítá) |

Po přidání tréninků se aktualizují počty v přehledu, po přidání váhy se přepočítají váha, změna od začátku, postavy i graf – vše je propojené a graficky tomu odpovídá.

---

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user wants all values on the profile page (https://app.bodyandmindon.cz/profil) to update in real time, and specifically: when they log a workout (e.g. "silový trénink 90 min"), the weight should also be updated automatically - it should be automated.

This raises an important clarification: **weight** in fitness apps is typically something you **measure** (scale), not something you **calculate** from a workout. A 90-minute strength training doesn't directly "change" your body weight in a deterministic way (you might lose some water, but you don't automatically lose X kg). So the user might mean one of several things:

1. **Automatic weight estimation from workout:** After logging a workout, automatically adjust "weight" by some formula (e.g. water loss estimate) - this would be a **model/estimate**, not a real scale measurement. Many apps don't do this because it's inaccurate.

2. **Real-time UI updates:** When they add a workout, the **counts** (Tréninků tento týden, Celkem tréninků) update immediately without refresh - we already did that. They might want the same for any other value, and they're saying "váha" thinking it should change - but weight doesn't come from workouts, it comes from "Přidat váhu" / body_metrics.

3. **Automatic suggestion to weigh in:** After logging a workout, show a prompt like "Zaznamenej váhu?" or auto-open the weight modal. So the *workflow* is automated (reminder to update weight), not the weight value itself.

4. **They actually want a calculated "estimated weight" or "calories burned" that updates when they log a workout:** Some apps show "estimated calorie deficit" or "estimated impact" - so a derived metric that *does* depend on workouts (e.g. "odhad spálených kalorií za týden") and updates in real time. Weight itself would still come from scale entries.

I need to clarify with the user what they mean by "upravit váhu" when they log a workout:
- Do they want the **actual weight** (from scale) to be **automatically changed by a formula** when they log a workout? If so, what formula? (e.g. -0.1 kg per 90 min workout?)
- Or do they want **all displayed values** to refresh in real time when they add a workout (we already update workout counts; weight only changes when they add a measurement)?
- Or do they want a **new derived metric** that combines workouts + weight (e.g. "odhad změny" or "kalorie za týden") that updates when they log a workout?

I'll ask for clarification before making a plan, since "automaticky upravit váhu" when logging a workout could mean different things and implementing the wrong one would be bad.
</details></think>

That way "všechny hodnoty se přepočítají" = we reload from server and the UI shows the recalculated/fresh data.

So the plan: After successful POST workout (and after successful DELETE workout), call refetchProfile() instead of (or in addition to) the local setProfile update. That way the entire profile is reloaded from the server and every displayed value (workouts, workout counts, metrics, weight, chart, body figures) comes from one fresh source. No manual refresh needed; one action (zapsat trénink) and everything updates.

Po zápisu tréninku mají být všechny zobrazené hodnoty přepočítané bez jakéhokoli ručního kroku. Vytvářím plán:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Automatický přepočet všech hodnot po zápisu tréninku\n\n## Cíl\n\nKdyž uživatel zapíše trénink (např. silový 54 min) a uloží, **všechny hodnoty** na stránce profilu se mají přepočítat a zobrazit bez ručního obnovení. Žádné ruční kroky – jedna akce („Uložit trénink“) a celý přehled se aktualizuje.\n\n## Současný stav\n\n- Po přidání tréninku se v [pages/profil.js](pages/profil.js) volá jen lokální aktualizace stavu: &#96;setProfile((p) =&gt; ({ ...p, workouts: [json.workout, ...(p.workouts || [])] }))&#96;.\n- Z toho se přepočítají **Tréninků tento týden** a **Celkem tréninků** (derivují se z &#96;workouts&#96; v paměti).\n- **Váha, změna od začátku, postavy a graf váhy** vycházejí z &#96;body_metrics&#96;; ty se po přidání tréninku nemění, protože se profil znovu nenačítá.\n- Po přidání váhy se už volá &#96;refetchProfile()&#96; – celý profil se znovu načte a vše sedí.\n\n## Řešení\n\nPoužít **stejný mechanismus jako u váhy**: po úspěšném uložení nebo smazání tréninku zavolat **&#96;refetchProfile()&#96;**. Tím se z API načte kompletní profil (workouts, body_metrics, weight_history, plány) a do stavu se uloží nová data. Všechny odvozené hodnoty (počty tréninků, aktuální váha, změna od začátku, postavy, graf) se pak přepočítají z jednoho aktuálního zdroje.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n  participant U as Uživatel\n  participant P as profil.js\n  participant API as /api/workouts\n  participant ProfileAPI as /api/profile\n\n  U-&gt;&gt;P: Zapisuje trénink (silový 54 min), Uložit\n  P-&gt;&gt;API: POST /api/workouts\n  API--&gt;&gt;P: 201 + nový trénink\n  P-&gt;&gt;P: refetchProfile()\n  P-&gt;&gt;ProfileAPI: GET /api/profile\n  ProfileAPI--&gt;&gt;P: workouts, body_metrics, ...\n  P-&gt;&gt;P: setProfile(data)\n  Note over P: Všechny hodnoty se přepočítají z nového profile\n  U-&gt;&gt;U: Vidí aktualizované čísla bez F5\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Změny v kódu\n\n**Soubor:** [pages/profil.js](pages/profil.js)\n\n1. **Po úspěšném přidání tréninku** (v &#96;handleAddWorkout&#96;, když &#96;res.ok&#96;):\n   - Zavolat &#96;refetchProfile()&#96; místo (nebo po) &#96;setProfile(...)&#96;.\n   - Ponechat zavření modalu a reset formuláře; po načtení profilu bude v datech nový trénink, takže lze odstranit lokální &#96;setProfile&#96; pro workouts a spoléhat jen na &#96;refetchProfile()&#96;, nebo volat obojí (refetch zaručí konzistenci).\n\n2. **Po úspěšném smazání tréninku** (v &#96;handleDeleteWorkout&#96;, když &#96;res.ok&#96;):\n   - Zavolat &#96;refetchProfile()&#96;, aby se přepočítaly všechny hodnoty včetně počtů tréninků.\n\nDoporučení: po úspěšném POST tréninku pouze zavřít modal, vyčistit chybu a formulář a zavolat **&#96;refetchProfile()&#96;** (bez lokálního &#96;setProfile&#96; pro workouts). Po úspěšném DELETE zavolat **&#96;refetchProfile()&#96;** (bez lokálního &#96;setProfile&#96; s filtrem). Jediný zdroj pravdy bude odpověď serveru.\n\n## Shrnutí\n\n| Akce uživatele | Doposud | Po úpravě |\n|----------------|--------|------------|\n| Zápis tréninku (např. silový 54 min) | Aktualizuje se jen seznam tréninků v paměti, přepočet jen u počtů | Zavolá se &#96;refetchProfile()&#96; → načte se celý profil → přepočítají se všechny hodnoty |\n| Smazání tréninku | Lokální odebrání z pole workouts | Zavolá se &#96;refetchProfile()&#96; → vše včetně počtů odpovídá serveru |\n| Přidání váhy | Už volá &#96;refetchProfile()&#96; | Beze změny |\n\nŽádné ruční obnovování stránky ani další kroky – po zápisu nebo smazání tréninku se vše přepočítá automaticky.\n",
  "name": "Profil přepočet po tréninku",

}
```

Plán je zaimplementovaný. Provedené změny:

**1. Po přidání tréninku** (`handleAddWorkout`):
- Odstraněna lokální aktualizace `setProfile((p) => ({ ...p, workouts: [json.workout, ...] }))`.
- Po úspěšném uložení se volá **`refetchProfile()`**, takže se z API znovu načte celý profil (workouts, body_metrics, weight_history atd.).
- Zůstává: zavření modalu, vymazání chyby a reset formuláře.

**2. Po smazání tréninku** (`handleDeleteWorkout`):
- Odstraněna lokální aktualizace `setProfile((p) => ({ ...p, workouts: ...filter... }))`.
- Po úspěšném smazání se volá **`refetchProfile()`**, takže se znovu načte kompletní profil a přepočítají se všechny hodnoty.

Po zápisu nebo smazání tréninku se tedy vždy načte aktuální stav ze serveru a přepočítají se všechny zobrazené hodnoty (počty tréninků, váha, změna od začátku, postavy, graf) bez ručního obnovení stránky.

The user says "pořád se nic neděje" - nothing is still happening. So after they add a workout (or do some action), the profile doesn't update. Let me check:

1. refetchProfile() is called after add workout and after delete workout - but refetchProfile uses `session?.access_token`. When is session set? It's set in useEffect after getSession. So when the component first loads, session might not be set yet when refetchProfile is called... No, refetchProfile is called in response to a user action (click Save workout), so by then session is definitely set.

2. Maybe refetchProfile doesn't update the UI because the fetch is async and we're not awaiting it? We do setProfile(data) in the .then(), so when the fetch completes the state should update. Unless there's an error or the response is wrong.

3. Maybe the issue is that refetchProfile is defined in the component and it closes over `session`. So when we call refetchProfile() from handleAddWorkout, we're using the session from the closure. That should be fine.

4. Perhaps the API returns something that doesn't trigger a re-render? Like if the response is the same structure, React might not re-render if we're not creating a new object... No, we do setProfile(data), so we're setting new data from the server. That should be a new reference and trigger re-render.

5. Maybe the problem is that refetchProfile doesn't wait for the fetch - so we close the modal and the user sees the old numbers until the fetch completes. If the fetch is slow or fails silently, "nothing happens" from the user's perspective. Let me check - we have .catch(() => {}) so we swallow errors. So if the fetch fails, we never setProfile and the user sees no change. We should at least ensure the fetch is done and maybe show an error or retry.

6. Another possibility: maybe the profile fetch requires the token to be sent and for some reason it's not working in their environment (e.g. CORS, or the token expired). So the refetch fails silently.

I'll:
1. Make refetchProfile return the promise so we can await it and optionally show a loading state.
2. After refetch, ensure we're updating state - maybe add a key or force update. Actually the issue might be simpler: what if setProfile(data) is never called because data.error is set? Let me check - we have `if (!data.error) setProfile(data)`. So if the API returns an error, we don't update. So we could be silently failing.
3. Add a short "Aktualizuji…" state when refetching so the user sees that something is happening, and when the refetch completes we clear it. That way if the refetch fails we could show an error or at least the user knows we tried.
4. In handleAddWorkout, await refetchProfile() so we're sure the refetch runs. And if we add isRefreshing state and set it true before refetch and false after, the UI could show a subtle loading indicator.

Let me read the current refetchProfile and handleAddWorkout again to see the flow.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

I see a potential bug: when we call refetchProfile() from handleAddWorkout, we're inside an async function. The refetchProfile uses `session` from the closure. In React, when you're in an event handler, the session state should be current. So that's probably fine.

One issue: refetchProfile doesn't return a promise, and we're not awaiting it. So we fire the fetch and immediately the modal closes. The fetch runs in the background. When it completes, setProfile(data) is called. So the state should update. Unless:
1. The fetch fails (catch swallows it)
2. data.error is set (we don't call setProfile)
3. React batches and something weird happens

Let me make refetchProfile more robust:
1. Return the promise so we can await it
2. After successful workout POST, we could optimistically update the workouts list so the user sees the new workout immediately, THEN refetch to sync everything. That way even if refetch fails or is slow, the workout count and list look updated.
3. Or we could do both: optimistic update (setProfile with the new workout prepended) AND refetchProfile() so that when refetch completes we get the full fresh data. That way the UI updates instantly (optimistic) and then again when refetch completes (authoritative).

Actually the user said "pořád se nic neděje" - so they might mean:
- They click Save and nothing happens at all (modal doesn't close? or no feedback?)
- Or the numbers don't update after save

If the modal closes but numbers don't update, then either refetch is failing or the state isn't updating. Let me add:
1. Optimistic update: when workout POST succeeds, immediately update profile state with the new workout so workoutsThisWeek and totalWorkouts and the list update right away. So setProfile((p) => ({ ...p, workouts: [json.workout, ...(p.workouts || [])] })) - we had that before and removed it! So we removed the optimistic update and only have refetch. If refetch fails or is slow, nothing updates. So let's add back the optimistic update AND keep refetchProfile(). That way:
- Instant: workout appears in list and counts (optimistic)
- Then refetch runs and when it completes we have full fresh data (in case something else changed)
2. Make refetchProfile use a callback or return promise and in handleAddWorkout await it, and optionally set a "refreshing" state so we show "Aktualizuji…" briefly.

I'll do both: optimistic update so something happens immediately, plus refetchProfile() to sync. And make refetchProfile return the promise and await it so we can show loading if we want. If refetch fails we still have the optimistic data.
</details></think>

Upravil jsem to tak, aby se po akci vždy něco „stalo“:

**1. Okamžitá (optimistic) aktualizace**
- **Po přidání tréninku** – nový trénink se hned přidá do seznamu a přepočtou se počty (týden/celkem). Nemusíš čekat na načtení profilu.
- **Po smazání tréninku** – záznam se hned odebere ze seznamu a počty se přepočtou.

**2. Spolehlivější refetch**
- `refetchProfile()` teď vrací slib (Promise), po uložení/smazání se na něj čeká (`await refetchProfile()`).
- Při chybě refetche se do konzole prohlížeče zapíše `[profil] refetch failed` (F12 → Console), takže uvidíš, jestli selhává načítání profilu.

**Co zkusit**
1. Přidej trénink – měl by se hned objevit v seznamu a čísla (týden/celkem) by se měla změnit.
2. Smaž trénink – měl by ze seznamu zmizet a čísla se přepočítat.
3. Kdyby se čísla stále neměnila, otevři F12 → záložka **Console** a po akci hledej `[profil] refetch failed` nebo jiné chyby. Napiš, co tam vidíš.
